blog_5signs.title: 5 Signs Your Small Business Website Needs a Redesign
blog_5signs.hero_lede: If your site is quietly costing you customers, it's usually one of these five problems.
blog_5signs.intro: A website doesn't have to be broken to be losing you business. Most of the sites we get called about still technically work — they just don't work hard enough. Here are five signs it's time for a redesign.
blog_5signs.h2_1: 1. It doesn't work well on a phone
blog_5signs.p1: More than half of web traffic for most small businesses comes from phones, not desktops. If your site was built years ago and never properly adapted for mobile — text too small, buttons too close together, images that don't resize — visitors will leave before they ever read your pitch.
blog_5signs.p2: If your homepage takes more than a few seconds to load, a real percentage of visitors are gone before they see it. Slow sites are usually the result of unoptimized images, outdated hosting, or bloated page builders — all fixable.
blog_5signs.h2_3: 3. There's no clear call to action
blog_5signs.p3: A visitor should never have to hunt for how to contact you or request a quote. If your phone number, email, and a "get a quote" button aren't visible within a few seconds of landing on the page, you're losing leads to confusion, not competition.
blog_5signs.p4: Design trends move fast. Heavy drop shadows, stock photo clip art, auto-playing music, or a layout that hasn't changed since 2015 all quietly signal "this business might not be around anymore" — even if you're thriving.
blog_5signs.h2_5: 5. You can't update it yourself (or it's terrifying to try)
blog_5signs.p5: If updating your hours, adding a new service, or swapping a photo means calling someone and waiting a week, that's a sign your site was built on the wrong platform for how you actually run your business.
blog_5signs.p6: None of these require starting from scratch. A redesign can often keep everything that's working and just fix what isn't — that's exactly what our Starter and Business website packages are built around.

about.hero_h1: One technician. No jargon. No games.
about.hero_lede: Little Technical Solutions LLC is a veteran-owned technology company serving homes and small businesses — websites, computers, networks, and security, handled by one point of contact.
about.mission_h2: Affordable, professional, and transparent — for the long haul.
about.mission_lede: Our mission is to provide honest technology services while building long-term relationships with our clients — the trusted local partner businesses can rely on for websites, IT support, networking, cybersecurity guidance, and whatever comes next.
about.why_h2: Small enough to know your setup. Sharp enough to fix all of it.
about.why_lede: You don't need three different vendors for your website, your Wi-Fi, and your antivirus. You need one technician who understands how they all connect — because they do.
about.why_card1_desc: The person who sets up your network is the same person who can fix your website. Nothing gets lost between vendors.
about.why_card2_desc: You'll know what was wrong, what we did, and what to watch for — no jargon, no condescension.
about.why_card3_desc: You approve the quote before we start. No hourly clock running while we figure things out.
about.why_card4_title: Security built in, not bolted on
about.why_card4_desc: Every job — from a new website to a new router — gets set up with security best practices from the start.
about.why_card5_desc: You're calling a neighbor, not a call center — we know the area because we live and work in it.
about.why_card6_title: Built around you, not a template
about.why_card6_desc: Every project is scoped to what your business actually needs, with room for custom requests beyond the standard packages.
about.why_card7_desc: Post-launch support comes standard, with ongoing help available through our Website Care Plan.
about.photo_console_alt: Dylan Little monitoring a command-and-control console aboard USS Antietam
about.photo_console_caption: Standing watch aboard USS Antietam (CG-54) — the same focus now goes into every website and network we build.
about.founder_lede: Little Technical Solutions LLC is proudly veteran-owned. Founder Dylan Little served six years in the U.S. Navy as a Fire Controlman, including four years aboard the guided-missile cruiser USS Antietam, maintaining high-voltage combat systems and command-and-control electronics — hands-on troubleshooting, repair, and testing where equipment failure wasn't an option. After the Navy, Dylan carried that same discipline into a career as a systems engineer while earning a Bachelor's degree in Cybersecurity Technology. He founded Little Technical Solutions to put that technical background to work for local businesses — building websites and handling IT the way he'd want it done for his own. We extend that same appreciation to others who serve — see our American Heroes Discount.
about.cred1_desc: Fire Controlman, 6 years — including 4 years of hands-on electrical & electronic systems maintenance aboard USS Antietam
about.photo_underway_alt: Dylan Little on deck during underway operations aboard USS Antietam
about.photo_pinning_alt: Dylan Little receiving a qualification pin aboard USS Antietam
about.stat2_label: Documented hours on Tomahawk system maintenance
about.stat4_label: Sailors supervised as Leading Petty Officer
about.step1_desc: Starter or Business — flat-rate pricing, no surprises. See Pricing.
about.step2_desc: Use the Website Designer tool to pick what you want and watch your starting price update in real time.
about.step3_desc: Work with Dylan directly — as hands-on or hands-off as you'd like — through one or two revision rounds before launch.
about.step4_desc: Every website launches with post-launch support built in, plus the option to keep it maintained long-term.
about.support_desc: Starter websites include 30 days of post-launch support; Business websites include 60 days. Want help beyond that? The Website Care Plan covers hosting coordination, small content updates, platform health checks, and general maintenance — so your site stays online and current without you having to manage it.
about.future_h2: Proprietary tools, built for our clients.
about.future_desc: We're actively building software to make owning a website easier — the Website Designer tool is the first of it. Website Care Plan subscribers get access to new tools like it as they're released, at no additional cost.
about.beyond_websites_note: Beyond websites, we also handle computer repair, networking, cybersecurity guidance, and general small business IT support — see the full lineup on Services.
about.letter_portrait_alt: Portrait of Dylan Little, founder of Little Technical Solutions LLC
about.letter_h2: Thank you for taking the time to learn about us.
about.letter_body: Every client places their trust in me, and I take that responsibility seriously. Whether you're launching your first business, modernizing an existing website, or simply need someone local to help with technology, I'd be honored to earn your business and build something we're both proud of.
about.new_business_note: We're a brand-new business — that means direct, personal attention on every job, without a queue of other clients ahead of you.
about.cta_p: Reach out directly or fill out our project form — we typically respond the same business day.

faq.lede: Answers to the questions we hear most. Don't see yours? Just ask.
faq.q1_answer: Computer repair, networking, and on-site cybersecurity work is available in person across Montross, Colonial Beach, King George, and Dahlgren, Virginia. Website design is different — it's fully remote, so we take on website clients anywhere in the United States. See our Service Area page for the full breakdown.
faq.q2_answer: Call 636-426-0289, email dylan@lit-solutions.tech, or fill out our project form — we typically respond the same business day.
faq.q3_answer: Yes — our Terms & Conditions cover payment terms, cancellations, ownership, and everything else in plain language before any project starts.
faq.q4_question: What's the difference between the Starter and Business website packages?
faq.q4_answer: Starter covers up to 5 pages with the essentials — contact form, mobile-responsive design, and basic SEO. Business covers up to 10 pages plus advanced features like individual service pages, booking, testimonials, FAQ, and a blog. See the full package comparison for the exact breakdown.
faq.q5_question: How long does a website project take?
faq.q5_answer: It depends on scope, but most Starter sites are completed in 1–2 weeks and Business sites in 2–4 weeks once we have your content and any photos. Timelines are confirmed upfront before work begins.
faq.q6_answer: Yes — one revision round is included on the Starter package and two on the Business package, so you can request changes before final launch.
faq.q7_question: What happens after my site launches?
faq.q7_answer: You get 30 days of post-launch support on Starter and 60 days on Business. After that, ongoing maintenance and hosting assistance is available as a monthly subscription if you want it.
faq.q8_question: I'm not in Virginia — can you still build my site?
faq.q8_answer: Yes. Website design is 100% remote from first call to launch. Your location doesn't matter for this service.
faq.q9_answer: We confirm your exact price before any work begins, then you pay through our secure Square payment page. Recurring services (subscriptions) are billed monthly through Square as well.
faq.q10_question: What is the American Heroes Discount?
faq.q10_answer: A 15% discount on one-time services (5% on recurring/subscription services) for active duty military, veterans, teachers, first responders, doctors, TSA agents, police, firefighters, and Virginia FFL holders. See Heroes Discount for eligibility and verification details.
faq.q11_question: Are your prices the final price?
faq.q11_answer: The prices on our pricing page are accurate starting points for typical jobs. Parts, extensive repairs, and larger custom projects are quoted individually and always confirmed with you before work begins — no surprise line items.

heroes.eligibility_note:
Proof of eligibility required.
Documents (DD-214, LES, or an employment verification letter): email a copy along with your project form.
Federal ID cards (military ID/CAC, veteran ID card, TSA credential, etc.): we verify these in person only — we can't accept a photo, scan, or copy, since federal law (18 U.S.C. § 701) prohibits reproducing military and other federal government ID cards.
We verify once; you get the discount on every job after that.

heroes.web_item5_name: Business email setup <small>(our labor fee — subscription billed by Google/Microsoft)</small>
heroes.web_item7_name: Ongoing maintenance & hosting assistance <small>(5% off — see note below)</small>

heroes.biz_item6_name: Ongoing IT support <small>(5% off — see note below)</small>
heroes.pricing_note:
One-time services: 15% off our standard starting price, shown above.
Recurring monthly services (ongoing IT support, website maintenance): 5% off, reflecting that these prices already run close to our actual cost of keeping your systems running — server and infrastructure costs behind these plans aren't discounted.
Parts and hardware (PC build components, mesh Wi-Fi units, etc.) are always billed at cost, with no markup and no discount, regardless of eligibility.
Domain registration and business email setup prices reflect only our labor — registrar fees and Google/Microsoft subscription costs are billed to you directly by those providers, not through us.
Prices shown here are starting points for typical jobs. We don't take payment on this page — once we've verified your eligibility, we'll confirm your exact discounted total and send you to our payment page to pay it.
heroes.cta_p: Fill out our project form and mention which category applies to you — we'll verify your eligibility, confirm your discounted price, and follow up with next steps for payment.

home.h1:
Every wire, every login, every website.
Handled by one call.
home.lede: Little Technical Solutions LLC is the technician you actually keep on speed dial — websites, computer repair, networking, and cybersecurity, done right the first time.
home.tagline: Modernizing the Northern Neck, One Small Business at a Time.
home.services_lede: Most IT shops make you find a different vendor for every problem. We keep it to one ticket, one visit, one point of contact.
home.nationwide_note: Computer repair, networking, and on-site security work are local — Montross, Colonial Beach, King George, and Dahlgren, Virginia. Website design is not. We build custom websites for small businesses anywhere in the country, entirely remote, start to finish.
home.step1_desc: Call, text, or send the contact form. Describe the problem — slow Wi-Fi, a site that needs rebuilding, a PC that won't boot. No jargon required.
home.step2_desc: We diagnose the issue and give you a clear, flat quote before any work starts. No surprise line items.
home.step3_title: We fix it — on-site or remote
home.step3_desc: Most computer and network jobs happen at your home or office. Website and cloud work can usually be handled remotely.
home.step4_desc: Ongoing maintenance, updates, and support are available so the fix stays fixed.
home.why_h2: Veteran-owned. Small enough to know your setup.
home.why_lede: Founder Dylan Little served six years in the U.S. Navy as a Fire Controlman, including four years aboard the guided-missile cruiser USS Antietam. That same discipline now goes into every website, network, and repair job — one point of contact, plain-language explanations, and flat, upfront pricing.
home.cred1_desc: Fire Controlman, 6 years — including 4 years of hands-on electrical & electronic systems maintenance aboard USS Antietam
home.cred3_desc: See our completed website build for Armour Wireless Solutions, plus current launch pricing for new projects.
home.contact_p: Reach out directly, or fill out our project form so we can get the full picture before we call. We typically respond the same business day.

Parts 3 and 4 only need to be filled out if you check the boxes that apply to them below; otherwise, feel free to skip past them (you can still open them to look, no pressure to fill them in). Section 5 at the very bottom is optional.

<svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.8"><path d="M12 9v4M12 17h.01M10.29 3.86L1.82 18a2 2 0 0 0 1.71 3h16.94a2 2 0 0 0 1.71-3L13.71 3.86a2 2 0 0 0-3.42 0z"/></svg>

payment.biz_plan_desc: Ongoing small-business IT support outside of one-off project work — a technician on call for the day-to-day stuff, without a per-visit invoice every time something comes up.
payment.biz_plan_vet_note: American Heroes Discount rate: $75/month — see eligibility & verification.
payment.subs_note: Subscriptions are billed monthly through Square and renew automatically until canceled. You can cancel either plan anytime with written notice (a call, text, or email works) before your next billing date — no long-term contract. Scope for Small Business IT Support is confirmed with you before your first billing cycle. Server and infrastructure costs behind these plans are not discounted, which is why the American Heroes Discount on subscriptions is 5% rather than 15%.
payment.final_note: Payments are securely processed by Square. Little Technical Solutions LLC does not collect, see, or store your card details. Refunds and disputes are handled per our standard service terms — contact us directly with any billing questions before opening a dispute with your bank or card issuer, so we can make it right.
payment.cta_h2: Not sure which one you need?
payment.cta_p: Fill out our project form and tell us what you're picturing — we'll help you figure out what makes sense.

portfolio.featured_item1_desc: Desktop, tablet, and mobile layouts built around Armour Wireless branding and a professional, Navy-inspired direction.
portfolio.featured_item2_desc: Home, company background, capabilities, contracting information, contact content, and clear navigation.
portfolio.featured_item3_desc: Client-provided information organized into readable, web-ready content for carrier and government-facing visitors.
portfolio.featured_item4_desc: Deployment support, domain coordination, click-to-call and click-to-email functions, testing, and revisions.
portfolio.featured_item5_desc: A protected client sign-in process with two-factor authentication and controlled access to management functions.
portfolio.featured_item6_desc: Responsive public gallery plus image upload and removal tools, storage integration, validation, and handoff guidance.
portfolio.featured_outcome: Delivered outcome: A clean, mobile-ready website that gives Armour Wireless one professional destination for its company story, capabilities, contracting information, contact options, and client-managed project photos — without requiring the client to edit code.
portfolio.featured_disclaimer: This project summary was prepared by Little Technical Solutions LLC from the completed project scope. It describes delivered work; it is not presented as a quotation or personal endorsement from the client.

pricing.hero_lede: Flat rates for common jobs, so you know the cost before we start. Every price below is a starting point — your exact quote depends on the specifics of the job, and we'll always confirm it with you first.
pricing.launch_banner: Launch special: the prices below are introductory rates for our first 25 customers as we get Little Technical Solutions LLC off the ground. Once we hit that milestone, pricing moves to our standard published rates.
pricing.packages_h2: Which website package is right for you?
pricing.packages_lede: Both packages include a custom design, mobile-responsive build, and basic SEO. The difference is scope — how many pages, and how much the site does on its own.
pricing.starter_tagline: A clean, professional site that gets you found and gets you contacted.
pricing.starter_price_note: A flat starting price, confirmed with you before any work begins.
pricing.starter_feature1: Up to 5 pages (Home, About, Services, Contact + 1 more)
pricing.starter_feature2: Custom design matched to your brand
pricing.business_tagline: A full site with the tools to actually run leads, bookings, and content through it.
pricing.business_price_note: A flat starting price, confirmed with you before any work begins.
pricing.business_feature1: Up to 10 pages, including individual service pages
pricing.business_feature7: Everything in Starter, plus dropdown navigation & enhanced branding
pricing.packages_note: Want to pick your exact features and see a starting price first? Try our Website Designer. Not sure which package fits at all? Fill out our project form and we'll recommend the right one — we'd rather undersell than oversell.
pricing.compare_lede: No hourly surprises and no vague estimates. Every price above is a real starting point for that job — you'll know your price before any work begins, and it won't change unless the scope does.
pricing.price_note: Prices shown are starting points for typical jobs. Parts, hardware, extensive repairs, travel outside our service area, and larger projects are quoted individually and confirmed with you before any work begins. Pricing is subject to change without notice; the rates above reflect what's currently in effect. No hidden fees, no surprise line items.
pricing.vet_h2: Serve your community? You get a discount, too.
pricing.vet_p: We offer 15% off nearly every service on this page for active duty military, veterans, teachers, first responders, doctors, TSA agents, police, firefighters, and Virginia FFL holders — a small thank-you for what you do.
pricing.cta_p: Fill out our project form and we'll follow up with a straightforward quote based on your specific situation.

privacy.hero_lede: Last updated: July 2026. What we collect, why, and how you can control it.
privacy.section1_intro: Little Technical Solutions LLC collects the following information, depending on how you use this site:
privacy.section1_item1: Contact & project forms. When you use the quick contact form, the project intake form, the Website Designer tool, or the newsletter signup, we receive the name, email, phone, and message/project details you submit — including, for the Website Designer, any logo or photo files you choose to upload.
privacy.section1_item2: Site analytics. We use Netlify Analytics, which is server-side and cookie-free — it reports aggregate traffic (pages visited, referring sites, rough location by country/region) without tracking individual visitors across sites or building an ad profile.
privacy.section1_item3: Local storage, not cookies. Your dark/light mode choice and whether you've dismissed the cookie notice are saved in your browser's local storage, not a cookie. Nothing here is used to track you across other websites.
privacy.section1_outro: We do not collect or store payment card information. Payments are processed directly by Square; see their own privacy policy for how they handle payment data.
privacy.section2_body: Information you submit is used to respond to your inquiry, scope and deliver the service you requested, operate your account (if you create one) and the features you use within it, and — only if you opted in — send the occasional blog update. We do not use your information for anything else, and we do not build advertising profiles.
privacy.section3_body: We do not sell your personal information. Your information is processed through a small number of service providers solely to deliver the service you requested: Netlify (our hosting, forms, and file-storage provider), Square (our payment processor), and Resend (our transactional email provider, used to send account verification, password reset, and message-notification emails). We do not share your information with anyone else except where required by law.
privacy.section4_body: This site does not set third-party advertising or tracking cookies. The cookie notice you may see on your first visit is a transparency notice, not a tracking opt-in — there's nothing nonessential to opt into beyond the analytics described above, which collects no personal data.
privacy.section5_body: We retain form submissions and project records for as long as reasonably necessary to deliver the service, maintain business records, and meet legal obligations. You can request access to, correction of, or deletion of your information at any time by contacting us at 636-426-0289 or dylan@lit-solutions.tech.
privacy.section6_body: This site is served entirely over HTTPS. We do not store payment card data. Form data is transmitted and stored through Netlify's infrastructure with reasonable technical safeguards.
privacy.section7_body: This site is intended for business owners and adults and is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect information from children.
privacy.section8_body: We may update this policy from time to time. The version in effect at the time you use this site or submit a form governs that use.
privacy.section9_body: Questions about this policy can be directed to Little Technical Solutions LLC at 636-426-0289 or dylan@lit-solutions.tech. See also our Terms & Conditions.
privacy.section1_item4: Customer accounts. If you create an account, we store your name, email address, language preference, and a securely hashed password — we never see or store your actual password in readable form.
privacy.section1_item5: Documents, messages, favorites & notifications. Once you have an account, we store the documents (such as invoices or receipts) shared with your account, the content of messages exchanged with our staff, items you save as favorites, and account-related notifications — all tied to your account and visible only to you and authorized staff.
privacy.section1_item6: IP addresses. We log the IP address associated with form submissions, account sign-ins, and other account actions. This is used to prevent abuse (for example, limiting how many requests one visitor can make in a short period) and is stored alongside the related inquiry or account activity.

search.hero_lede: Search every page, service, and article on the site.

service_area.hero_lede: On-site computer, network, and security service is local to the Northern Neck of Virginia. Website design is available to small businesses anywhere in the country.
service_area.inperson_lede: Computer repair, networking, and on-site cybersecurity work is available in person across these communities:
service_area.town1_desc: Westmoreland County seat — home base for on-site visits.
service_area.town2_desc: On-site computer, network, and security service.
service_area.town3_desc: On-site computer, network, and security service.
service_area.town4_desc: On-site computer, network, and security service, including NSF Dahlgren-area homes and small businesses.
service_area.outside_towns_note: Outside these towns but nearby? Reach out anyway — some jobs are still workable depending on distance and scope.
service_area.remote_note: Website design works differently. Building, redesigning, and maintaining a website doesn't require us to be in the same room — so we take on website clients anywhere in the United States, fully remote, from first consultation to launch and ongoing support. If you're a small business owner outside Virginia looking for a website, reach out — location isn't a barrier.
service_area.cta_h2: Not sure if we cover your area?
service_area.cta_p: Reach out and ask — for on-site work we'll tell you honestly if it's outside a reasonable range.

service_business_it.hero_lede: One ongoing IT relationship instead of juggling five vendors when something breaks.
service_business_it.card1_desc: Technology consulting, workstation setup, and ongoing support all come from the same technician.
service_business_it.card2_desc: Ongoing IT support is a flat monthly rate, not a surprise invoice.
service_business_it.card3_desc: Email migration, domain management, and workstation setup scale as you add people.
service_business_it.full_list_note: Small Business IT services start at $39–$79. See the full pricing page for details.
service_business_it.step1_desc: We look at your current setup and where the recurring pain points are.
service_business_it.step2_desc: Workstations, printers, email, and domains configured and documented.
service_business_it.step3_desc: Available as a flat monthly plan — see our subscription options.
service_business_it.faq1_question: Do you offer an ongoing support plan?
service_business_it.faq1_answer: Yes — see our Small Business IT Support subscription for flat monthly pricing.
service_business_it.faq2_question: Can you migrate our business email?
service_business_it.faq2_answer: Yes — email migration & setup starts at $79 and covers moving to Google Workspace or Microsoft 365.
service_business_it.cta_h2: Ready to get started with Small Business IT?

service_computer.hero_lede: From a machine that won't boot to a full custom build — diagnosed right the first time instead of guessing and re-billing.
service_computer.included_card1_desc: We tell you what's actually wrong before you pay for anything.
service_computer.included_card2_desc: You approve the quote before we touch your machine.
service_computer.included_card3_desc: Backup and recovery assistance is available before any repair that risks your files.
service_computer.full_list_note: Computer repair starts at $59/hr (1-hr minimum); most flat-rate services start between $69–$120. See the full pricing page for the complete list.
service_computer.process_step1_desc: Bring it in or we come to you — we figure out what's actually wrong at no charge.
service_computer.process_step2_desc: You get a clear price before any repair work starts.
service_computer.process_step3_desc: Most repairs are same-day or next-day, depending on parts.
service_computer.process_step4_desc: We explain what was wrong and what to watch for going forward.
service_computer.faq1_answer: Yes — no charge to tell you what's wrong. You only pay if you decide to move forward with the repair.
service_computer.faq2_answer: Yes — labor starts at $120 plus the cost of parts, which are always billed at cost with no markup.
service_computer.cta_h2: Ready to get started with Computer Services?

service_cybersecurity.hero_lede: Most breaches start with a weak password or no MFA. We lock down the basics that stop the majority of real-world attacks.
service_cybersecurity.included_card1_desc: Founder Dylan Little holds a B.S. in Cybersecurity Technology from University of Maryland Global Campus.
service_cybersecurity.included_card2_desc: We focus on the handful of changes that actually reduce your real-world risk.
service_cybersecurity.included_card3_desc: Plain-language security reviews — what's actually at risk, and what to do about it.
service_cybersecurity.full_list_pricing_note: Security services start at $45–$99. See the full pricing page for details.
service_cybersecurity.process_step1_desc: We assess your current setup — accounts, devices, and network — for the highest-risk gaps.
service_cybersecurity.process_step2_desc: Password manager and MFA setup across your key accounts.
service_cybersecurity.process_step3_desc: A short, plain-language walkthrough of the habits that actually matter.
service_cybersecurity.faq1_question: I'm not a business — is this just for companies?
service_cybersecurity.faq1_answer: No — home and small business security reviews are both available, starting at $99.
service_cybersecurity.faq2_answer: Configuring multi-factor authentication on your important accounts (email, banking, business logins) so a stolen password alone isn't enough to get in.
service_cybersecurity.cta_h2: Ready to get started with Cybersecurity?

service_networking.hero_lede: Dead zones, dropped connections, and devices that won't talk to each other usually come down to one or two fixable settings.
service_networking.included_card1_desc: Mesh Wi-Fi installation eliminates dead zones instead of just moving them around.
service_networking.included_card2_desc: Small office network setups built for reliability, not just a router out of the box.
service_networking.included_card3_desc: You'll know why it was slow, not just that it's faster now.
service_networking.full_list_pricing_note: Networking services start at $49–$149 depending on scope. See the full pricing page for details.
service_networking.process_step1_desc: We look at your space and your devices to find where the connection is actually breaking down.
service_networking.process_step2_desc: Reconfigure existing hardware, or install new mesh/router hardware if needed.
service_networking.process_step3_desc: We verify coverage and speed in every room or work area before calling it done.
service_networking.faq1_answer: We can, or we can work with hardware you already own. Hardware is always billed at cost with no markup.
service_networking.faq2_question: Can you set up a small office network?
service_networking.faq2_answer: Yes — small office network setup starts at $149 and includes multi-device configuration.
service_networking.cta_h2: Ready to get started with Networking?

service_website.hero_lede: Custom websites that load fast and actually bring in calls — built, hosted, and maintained by the same person who answers the phone.
service_website.included_card1_desc: Every site is designed around one job: turning visitors into calls, emails, and quote requests.
service_website.included_card2_desc: Most of your customers are finding you on a phone. Every build is responsive from the ground up.
service_website.included_card3_desc: No lock-in to a proprietary builder — your domain, your content, your site.
service_website.full_list_pricing_note: Starter websites start at $699, Business websites start at $1,299. See the full pricing page for package details and current launch pricing.
service_website.process_step1_desc: We talk through what your site needs to do, and who it needs to do it for.
service_website.process_step2_desc: We design and build against a fixed, agreed-upon scope — Starter or Business tier.
service_website.process_step3_desc: You review before it goes live. Two rounds of revisions are included on the Business package.
service_website.process_step4_desc: 30–60 days of post-launch support included, with optional ongoing maintenance after that.
service_website.faq1_question: Do you build websites for businesses outside Virginia?
service_website.faq1_answer: Yes. Website design is fully remote — we've built this specifically so it can serve small businesses anywhere in the country, not just the Northern Neck.
service_website.faq2_question: What's the difference between Starter and Business packages?
service_website.faq2_answer: See the full package comparison for the exact feature breakdown between the two tiers.
service_website.faq3_question: Do I need to already have a domain?
service_website.faq3_answer: No — we can register one for you, or connect an existing one you already own.
service_website.cta_h2: Ready to get started with Website Services?

services.hero_lede: Most IT shops make you find a different vendor for every problem. We keep it to one ticket, one visit, one point of contact.
services.nationwide_note: Computer repair, networking, and on-site security work are local — Montross, Colonial Beach, King George, and Dahlgren, Virginia. Website design is not. We build custom websites for small businesses anywhere in the country, entirely remote, start to finish. See our full service area for details.
services.web_detail: A slow or dated site is a 24/7 first impression problem. We design, build, and host sites that load fast and actually bring in calls — see the before/after below.
services.pc_detail: From a machine that won't boot to a full custom build, we diagnose it right the first time instead of guessing and re-billing.
services.net_detail: Dead zones, dropped connections, and devices that won't talk to each other usually come down to one or two fixable settings.
services.sec_detail: Most breaches start with a weak password or no MFA. We lock down the basics that stop the majority of real-world attacks.
services.biz_detail: One ongoing IT relationship instead of juggling five vendors when something breaks.
services.showcase_lede: This is the actual gap between a site that's quietly losing you customers and one that's built to convert. Drag the handle.
services.showcase_before_banner: CLICK HERE!! ★ BEST PRICES ★ CALL NOW ★
services.showcase_handle_aria: Drag to compare before and after website
services.cta_h2: Not sure which service you need?
services.cta_p: Fill out our project form and we'll follow up with a straightforward answer.

sitemap.hero_lede: A plain list of everything, grouped by section. Looking for something specific? Try search instead.

team.hero_lede: Little Technical Solutions LLC is currently a team of one — which means the person who answers your call is the same person who does the work.
team.founder_bio: U.S. Navy veteran (Fire Controlman, 6 years, USS Antietam) and B.S. in Cybersecurity Technology from the University of Maryland Global Campus. Handles every website build, computer repair, network setup, and security job personally — one point of contact from first call to finished work.
team.growth_note: As Little Technical Solutions LLC grows, this page will grow with it. For now, every job — website, repair, network, or security — is handled directly by Dylan, not handed off to a subcontractor.

terms.hero_lede: Last updated: July 2026. Please read these terms carefully before purchasing any service from Little Technical Solutions LLC.
terms.section1_body: These Terms & Conditions ("Terms") form a binding agreement between you ("Client," "you") and Little Technical Solutions LLC ("we," "us," "our," "the Company"), a Virginia limited liability company. By requesting a quote, submitting a project form, scheduling service, or making a payment, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use our services or submit payment.
terms.section2_body: Little Technical Solutions LLC provides website design and development, computer repair, networking, cybersecurity, and small business IT services ("Services") as described on our website. Specific scope, deliverables, and pricing for any given job are confirmed with you individually before work begins. Prices published on our website are starting points for typical jobs and are not binding quotes for atypical, complex, or larger-scope work.
terms.section3_intro: Payment is processed securely through Square, via our website's payment page at lit-solutions.tech/payment. We do not collect, view, or store your payment card information. All payments require agreeing to these Terms via the checkbox on that page before the payment button will work. Unless otherwise agreed in writing:
terms.section3_li1: Fixed-price services (such as website packages) require full payment before work begins. We do not use a deposit-plus-balance structure — the full project amount is due upfront, before any work is authorized to start.
terms.section3_li2: Hourly and diagnostic-based services are billed upon completion of work, unless a different schedule is specified on your invoice.
terms.section3_li3: All prices are in U.S. dollars. You are responsible for any taxes applicable to your purchase.
terms.section3_li4: Payment is due upon receipt of invoice unless a different due date is specified.
terms.section3_li5: Late payments may result in suspension of ongoing services (such as website hosting assistance or monthly IT support) until the account is brought current.
terms.section4_p1: Because fixed-price project work (such as website design) begins with planning, design, setup, configuration, and custom labor immediately after payment, your payment may become partially or fully non-refundable once work has commenced. Any refund is limited to work not yet performed and is determined by Little Technical Solutions LLC in good faith based on completed labor and incurred costs. If you cancel before any work has begun, your payment may be refunded at our sole discretion, less any transaction fees already incurred.
terms.section4_p2: Completed, delivered work (including but not limited to finished websites, completed repairs, and completed installations) is non-refundable. If you believe work was not completed as agreed, you must contact us directly at 636-426-0289 or dylan@lit-solutions.tech within 7 days of completion so we can address the issue directly.
terms.section4_p3: You agree to contact us and attempt to resolve any billing dispute directly before initiating a chargeback, dispute, or reversal with your bank, card issuer, or Square. Initiating a chargeback for services that were actually rendered, without first attempting to resolve the matter with us, may be treated as fraudulent and pursued to the fullest extent permitted by law, including recovery of the disputed amount, collection costs, and reasonable attorney's fees. We retain all records of communications, project scope, and delivered work to contest unwarranted disputes.
terms.section5_li1: You are the lawful owner of, or have obtained express permission to authorize service on, any device, network, account, or system you ask us to work on.
terms.section5_li2: Any content, images, text, logos, or materials you provide for website work are either owned by you or properly licensed for your use, and do not infringe any third party's rights.
terms.section5_li3: Information you provide to us (including on the project intake form) is accurate and complete to the best of your knowledge.
terms.section5_li4: You are responsible for backing up your own data before any computer, network, or website service begins. While we take reasonable care, we are not responsible for data loss during repair, migration, or configuration work, except where caused by our gross negligence or willful misconduct.
terms.section6_body: Technology services inherently involve uncertainty. We will use reasonable skill and care consistent with industry standards, but we do not guarantee that any specific issue will be fully resolved, that hardware will be recoverable, that a device will function like new, or that a website will achieve any particular ranking, traffic, or business outcome. Diagnostic time and labor already performed are billable regardless of whether an issue is ultimately resolved, except where we are unable to perform the service at all due to our own fault.
terms.section7_body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Little Technical Solutions LLC's total liability for any claim arising out of or relating to our Services — whether in contract, tort, negligence, or otherwise — shall not exceed the total amount you paid us for the specific service giving rise to the claim. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive damages, including but not limited to lost profits, lost data, or business interruption, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages.
terms.section8_body: Some services involve third-party products or subscriptions not billed through us, including but not limited to domain registration fees, Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 subscriptions, hosting fees, hardware components, and software licenses. You are responsible for these costs directly with the applicable third party unless explicitly stated otherwise in your quote. We are not responsible for the acts, omissions, pricing changes, or service interruptions of third-party providers.
terms.section9_body: Upon full payment for a website project, you own the final website files and design delivered to you, excluding any third-party licensed assets (such as stock photography, fonts, or plugins), which remain subject to their original licenses. We are not responsible for maintaining, updating, or securing a website after delivery unless you have a separate, active maintenance agreement with us.
terms.section10_body: The American Heroes Discount is offered at our sole discretion to active duty military, veterans, teachers, first responders, doctors, TSA agents, police, firefighters, and Virginia-based Federal Firearms License (FFL) holders who provide valid proof of eligibility. Documents such as a DD-214, Leave and Earnings Statement (LES), employment verification letter, or current FFL may be submitted by email. Federal government-issued ID cards (including military ID/CAC, veteran ID card, and TSA credentials) must be verified in person — consistent with 18 U.S.C. § 701, we do not accept and will not request a photograph, scan, or copy of these cards. We reserve the right to verify eligibility, deny, or revoke the discount at any time if proof cannot be confirmed or is found to be inaccurate. Misrepresenting eligibility for this discount, under any category, may be treated as fraud.
terms.section11_body: We will take reasonable precautions to protect any personal or business information you share with us in the course of providing Services. We do not sell your personal information. We may need to access accounts, files, or systems to perform requested work; you authorize such access solely for the purpose of completing the agreed-upon Services.
terms.section12_body: You agree to indemnify and hold harmless Little Technical Solutions LLC, its owner, and any subcontractors from any claims, damages, losses, or expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) arising from: (a) your breach of these Terms; (b) content or materials you provided that infringe a third party's rights; (c) your unauthorized use of any device, account, or system serviced by us; or (d) fraudulent or bad-faith conduct, including unwarranted payment disputes for services actually rendered.
terms.section13_body: We are not liable for delays or failure to perform due to causes beyond our reasonable control, including but not limited to acts of God, severe weather, power or internet outages, third-party service outages, or other circumstances outside our control.
terms.section14_body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia, without regard to conflict-of-law principles. Any dispute arising from these Terms or our Services shall be resolved in the state or federal courts located in Virginia, and you consent to the jurisdiction of those courts.
terms.section15_body: We may update these Terms from time to time. The version in effect at the time you submit payment or request service governs that transaction. Continued use of our Services after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.
terms.section16_body: If any provision of these Terms is found unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect.
terms.section17_body: Questions about these Terms can be directed to Little Technical Solutions LLC at 636-426-0289 or dylan@lit-solutions.tech.

testimonials.hero_lede: Real feedback from real projects — not stock quotes, not stand-ins.
testimonials.empty_h2: More reviews are on the way.
testimonials.empty_desc: We publish a review once a client has shared it and approved the exact wording — more will appear here as they come in. In the meantime, see our completed project on the Portfolio page, or reach out directly to ask about our work.
testimonials.verify_h2: Here's what you can verify yourself
testimonials.cred1_desc: 6 years U.S. Navy, Fire Controlman — see our full story.
testimonials.cred2_desc: B.S. Cybersecurity Technology, University of Maryland Global Campus.
testimonials.cred3_desc: See our published rates — no surprise line items.
testimonials.hero_lede_hascontent: Real feedback from real projects -- not stock quotes, not stand-ins.
testimonials.review1_quote: Little Technical Solutions LLC built a professional website that gives Armour Wireless Solutions a clear online presence and accurately presents our background, capabilities, and contracting information. Dylan worked directly with me, communicated throughout the project, and made the changes I requested. After the original site was delivered, he also developed a secure photo-management feature that allows me to sign in with two-factor authentication and upload or remove website photos without editing code. I appreciated the straightforward process, personal attention, and willingness to build around what my company actually needed. I would recommend Little Technical Solutions LLC to other small businesses looking for professional website development and responsive, direct support.
testimonials.review1_author: Bill Armour, President / Owner, Armour Wireless Solutions, LLC

wd.hero_h1: Design your website. See your price. In minutes.
wd.hero_lede: Pick a package, check off the features you want, and submit your project. This gives us a running starting estimate as you go -- your final quote is always confirmed by us before any work begins.
wd.launch_banner: Launch special: the prices in this tool are introductory rates for our first 25 customers as we get Little Technical Solutions LLC off the ground. Once we hit that milestone, pricing moves to our standard published rates -- but the bundle discount and American Heroes Discount are permanent and will still apply. All current discounts stack.
wd.browser_empty_text: Your website will grow here as you build it.
wd.heroes_toggle_text: I self-attest that I qualify for the American Heroes Discount (15% off one-time work) — eligibility is confirmed with you directly before your final price is set
wd.step1_illustrative_note: Example estimate only -- final scope and price are confirmed by us after you submit.
wd.starter_tagline: A clean, professional site that gets you found and gets you contacted.
wd.starter_feature1: Custom design matched to your brand
wd.business_tagline: A full site with the tools to actually run leads, bookings, and content through it.
wd.included_note: These come standard -- nothing to select.
wd.optional_features_note: Check anything you'd like added. We'll confirm the final scope and price with you.
wd.premium_addons_note: Bigger capabilities, scoped and priced separately -- check anything you'd like a quote for.
wd.step3_note: Just your contact info -- no long form required. We'll follow up, and you can add full project details now or later.
wd.quote_recap_note: Starting price -- confirmed by us before any work begins.
wd.consent_text: I understand this is a starting estimate, not a final quote, and I agree to be contacted about my project. See our Privacy Policy.
wd.prompt_title: Want to finish your project details now?
wd.prompt_message: The more we know up front, the faster we can start once you're ready -- but it's completely optional. We'll follow up either way using the contact method you picked.
wd.prompt_new_tab_note: This opens a separate project details worksheet in a new browser tab -- your quote request has already been sent either way.
wd.prompt_popup_blocked: Your browser blocked the new tab.
wd.worksheet_opened_title: Your project worksheet opened in a new tab.
wd.worksheet_opened_message: Your quote request has already been received. Finish the worksheet whenever you're ready -- it stays open in that tab.
wd.step4_note: This is what actually lets us start building -- it gets turned into a project summary PDF and sent straight to us.
wd.form_domain_label: Current website or domain (if you have one)
wd.form_desired_domain_label: If you don't have a domain yet, what name would you like? (optional -- we can register it for you)
wd.brief_intro_note: This is what actually lets us start building -- without it we have to follow up before any work can begin.
wd.brief_description_label: What does your business do, and who are your customers? *

blog_password_manager.breadcrumb_current: Why Every Small Business Should Use a Password Manager
blog_password_manager.hero_h1: Why Every Small Business Should Use a Password Manager
blog_password_manager.hero_lede: The single highest-impact, lowest-cost security upgrade most small businesses haven't made yet.
blog_password_manager.p1: If we had to pick one security change that gives small businesses the best return for the least effort, it's this: get a password manager, and use it for every account.
blog_password_manager.p2: Most account breaches don't happen because of some sophisticated hacking technique. They happen because a password got reused across multiple sites, and one of those sites got breached. The attacker then tries that same email-and-password combination everywhere else — a technique called credential stuffing — and it works far more often than it should.
blog_password_manager.p3: If you (or your employees) reuse passwords, one breach anywhere becomes a breach everywhere.
blog_password_manager.h2_what_it_does: What a password manager actually does
blog_password_manager.p4: A password manager generates and stores a unique, long, random password for every single account you have, and automatically fills it in when you log in. You only need to remember one master password. That's it — that's the whole value proposition.
blog_password_manager.h2_why_matters: Why this matters more for small businesses
blog_password_manager.p5: Larger companies often have IT departments enforcing password policies. Small businesses usually don't — which means the decision to reuse "Business2024!" across your email, your bank, and your point-of-sale system is one employee's habit away from being a real problem.
blog_password_manager.p6: Most password managers take under 30 minutes to set up for a small team, and many have free tiers that are more than sufficient. Pair it with multi-factor authentication on your most important accounts (email and banking, at minimum), and you've closed off the two most common ways small businesses actually get breached.
blog_password_manager.p7: We set this up as a standalone service, or as part of any Cybersecurity or Small Business IT engagement.
blog_password_manager.related_card1_title: 5 Signs Your Small Business Website Needs a Redesign
blog_password_manager.related_card2_title: Wi-Fi Dead Zones: What's Actually Causing Them

blog_wifi_deadzones.breadcrumb_current: Wi-Fi Dead Zones: What's Actually Causing Them
blog_wifi_deadzones.hero_h1: Wi-Fi Dead Zones: What's Actually Causing Them
blog_wifi_deadzones.hero_lede: It's rarely your internet plan. Here's what's usually really going on.
blog_wifi_deadzones.intro_p1: "My Wi-Fi is bad in the back room" is one of the most common calls we get — and almost never because of the internet plan itself. Here's what's actually going on most of the time.
blog_wifi_deadzones.section1_h2: It's a coverage problem, not a speed problem
blog_wifi_deadzones.section1_p1: A single router, no matter how expensive, has a limited range. Walls, floors, metal appliances, and even fish tanks absorb or reflect Wi-Fi signals. If a room is far from the router or has a lot of obstructions between it and your devices, you'll get a weak or dropped connection regardless of how fast your internet plan is.
blog_wifi_deadzones.section2_h2: Router placement matters more than router price
blog_wifi_deadzones.section2_p1: A $300 router in a closet in the corner of the house will often perform worse than a $100 router placed centrally and up off the floor. Before spending money on new hardware, moving the router itself is worth trying first.
blog_wifi_deadzones.section3_h2: Too many devices, one access point
blog_wifi_deadzones.section3_p1: Modern households and small offices often have 15–30+ connected devices — phones, laptops, printers, smart TVs, thermostats, security cameras. A single router can get overwhelmed, especially older models. This shows up as slowdowns during peak use rather than a total dead zone.
blog_wifi_deadzones.section4_h2: The real fix for larger spaces: mesh Wi-Fi
blog_wifi_deadzones.section4_p1: For homes or offices where one router just can't reach every corner, a mesh Wi-Fi system — multiple small units working together to blanket the space — is almost always the right fix, rather than a single more powerful router. It solves the coverage problem at its root instead of trying to push one signal further than physics really allows.
blog_wifi_deadzones.closing_p1: If you're dealing with dead zones, dropped calls, or a network that only works "if you stand in the right spot," it's usually a quick diagnosis and a straightforward fix.
